title: EIC Accelerator 2026
geography: EU and Horizon Europe associated countries
amount: Grant up to EUR 2.5M plus equity investment for scale-up financing
eligibilitySummary: Single startups and SMEs with high-risk, high-impact innovations that can scale in Europe and globally.
description: A highly competitive EU instrument for breakthrough innovations that need grant support, investment, or blended finance to move from validated technology toward market scale.
whoCanApply: Single startups or SMEs established in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe associated country
Applicants with a high-risk innovation and credible route to international scale
Teams able to explain technology, market, IP, and commercialization strategy
whoCannotApply: Projects with routine product development and limited innovation risk
Large companies applying alone
Companies that cannot show credible ownership or access to the core technology
eligibleCosts: Prototype validation, pilots, testing, certification, and scale-up preparation
Commercialization and investment-readiness work where aligned with the call
sourceNotes: EIC funding page lists Accelerator 2026 as open with 2026 cut-off dates.

title: EIC STEP Scale Up
geography: EU and Horizon Europe associated countries
amount: Equity investments around EUR 10M to EUR 30M to catalyze large funding rounds
eligibilitySummary: Scaleups with strategic deep-tech innovations and investor pre-commitments for large funding rounds.
description: A scale-up investment route for companies that can become global leaders in strategic technologies and need major EU-backed equity support.
whoCanApply: High-growth SMEs, startups, small mid-caps, or eligible scaleups
Companies with strategic technologies and credible investor interest
Teams preparing large investment rounds
whoCannotApply: Idea-stage teams
Companies without scale-up traction or serious investor engagement
Projects that are not tied to strategic technology sectors
evaluationCriteria: Ability to catalyze a larger investment round
sourceNotes: EIC funding page lists STEP Scale Up as open with 2026 cut-off dates.

title: Eurostars September 2026 Call
geography: Participating Eurostars countries, including Bulgaria through national funding bodies
amount: National funding rates apply; Bulgarian SMEs may receive public support for eligible R&D costs when national budget is available
eligibilitySummary: Innovative SMEs leading international collaborative R&D projects with partners from participating countries.
description: Eurostars funds market-oriented international R&D projects led by innovative SMEs. It is strongest for startups that need cross-border technical collaboration and can define a product, process, or service with commercial potential.
whoCanApply: Innovative SMEs leading an international consortium
Consortia with independent partners from at least two participating countries
Projects with civilian, market-oriented R&D and clear commercialization potential
whoCannotApply: Applicants without an international partner
Projects without meaningful R&D work
Pure sales, distribution, or routine implementation projects
eligibleCosts: Materials and subcontracting where allowed nationally
sourceNotes: Eureka publishes Eurostars calls and national funding information by country.

title: EIT Venture Incubation Program
provider: European Institute of Innovation and Technology
geography: EU and eligible European innovation ecosystems
amount: Mentoring, venture-building support, ecosystem access, and possible follow-on opportunities
eligibilitySummary: Early teams and startups developing digital or innovation-led ventures that need incubation and market validation.
description: An EIT opportunity suited to early founders who need structured incubation, mentor access, and validation before pursuing larger grants or investment.
whoCanApply: Early-stage startup teams
Founders with a technology-enabled venture idea
Teams able to commit to the incubation timeline
whoCannotApply: Mature scaleups looking only for large grant funding
Projects unrelated to innovation or digital business creation
Teams without a clear problem, customer, or proposed solution
sourceNotes: EIT opportunity listing shows Venture Incubation Program with a 9 June 2026 deadline.

title: EIT Urban Mobility Financial Support To Startups Open Call 2026
geography: EU and Horizon Europe associated countries
amount: Startup capital and support package for selected urban mobility startups
eligibilitySummary: European mobility startups with scalable solutions and evidence of pilots, MVP, or market traction.
description: A sector-focused EIT route for startups improving urban mobility, logistics, electrification, shared transport, mobility data, and related climate or digital infrastructure.
whoCanApply: Startups incorporated in eligible European countries
Companies building urban mobility solutions
Teams with an MVP, pilot, or early traction
whoCannotApply: Companies outside the mobility scope
Pure consulting or non-scalable service projects
Teams without a product or pilot path
eligibleCosts: Pilots with cities or mobility partners
sourceNotes: EIT opportunity listing shows Financial Support To Startups Open Call 2026 with a 16 November 2026 deadline.

title: BG16RFPR001-1.010 New Models in Production by SMEs
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
amount: Procedure budget EUR 96.3M; company aid depends on enterprise category and aid regime
eligibilitySummary: Bulgarian micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises introducing new or improved production, process, goods, or service models.
description: A Bulgarian grant procedure for SMEs implementing new models in production or business processes. It can fit tech startups with existing revenue and a concrete investment project for software, equipment, patents, designs, or licenses.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises
Companies registered by the procedure's required reference date
Applicants with sufficient recent revenue and a project implemented in Bulgaria
whoCannotApply: Idea-stage founders without a registered SME
Applicants without the required sales history
Projects that do not introduce a new model at company level
eligibleCosts: Patents, utility models, industrial designs, and licenses
Selected employment costs where allowed by the procedure
evaluationCriteria: Fit with Innovation Strategy for Smart Specialisation areas
Quality of the new product, service, or process model
sourceNotes: ISUN and MIG sources list the procedure under PCIE; public summaries list the current 27 May 2026 deadline.

title: BG16RFPR002-1.018 Support for Bulgarian Innovative Enterprises with Seal of Excellence Projects
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / RIDST 2021-2027
amount: National grant support for Bulgarian companies with qualifying Seal of Excellence projects
eligibilitySummary: Bulgarian innovative enterprises with projects that received an EIC Seal of Excellence quality label.
description: A Bulgarian national support route for companies whose strong EU innovation proposals received a Seal of Excellence but need national funding to execute the project.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian innovative enterprises
Applicants holding a relevant Seal of Excellence
Companies able to implement the project in line with national procedure rules
whoCannotApply: Applicants without a qualifying Seal of Excellence
Projects that materially differ from the awarded proposal
Companies that fail national eligibility checks
eligibleCosts: Project activities consistent with the Seal of Excellence proposal
Eligible national budget categories defined in ISUN documents
sourceNotes: EUMIS page lists the procedure and 22 June 2026 deadline.

title: VISA-1.001 Startup Visa Certificate for High-Tech or Innovative Project
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / ISUN
amount: Certificate route supporting long-term residence permit applications; no direct grant funding
eligibilitySummary: Non-EU founders seeking a Bulgarian certificate for a high-tech or innovative project as part of a residence pathway.
description: A Bulgarian administrative support procedure that certifies high-tech or innovative projects for non-EU founders. It does not provide funding, but it can help foreign founders establish and operate an innovative business in Bulgaria.
whoCanApply: Third-country founders outside the EU
Founders proposing a high-tech or innovative project in Bulgaria
Applicants able to submit the required documents through ISUN
whoCannotApply: Applicants seeking direct grant funding
Projects without a credible innovation or high-tech basis
EU citizens who do not need this residence route
eligibleCosts: Administrative preparation and company setup are the founder's responsibility
requiredDocuments: Evidence of high-tech or innovative nature
sourceNotes: EUMIS page lists application period from 2 January 2026 to 31 December 2029.

title: BG16RFPR001-1.012 Digitalization of Enterprises
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
amount: BGN 5,000 to BGN 50,000 grant aid, up to 70 percent intensity
eligibilitySummary: Closed Bulgarian procedure for micro and small enterprises investing in ICT, digital marketing, ERP, CRM, and cybersecurity solutions.
description: A closed but useful reference procedure for Bulgarian digitalization grants. Similar future calls are likely to require clear SME eligibility, specific ICT purchases, and simple cost justification.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian micro and small enterprises meeting the procedure rules
Companies purchasing eligible ICT and digitalization solutions
Applicants able to apply through ISUN before the deadline
whoCannotApply: Companies outside Bulgaria
Medium or large enterprises if excluded by the procedure
Projects without eligible ICT or cybersecurity/digitalization activity
eligibleCosts: Production, logistics, and management process optimization tools
evaluationCriteria: Fit of ICT solution to business needs
sourceNotes: MIG page tracks contracts and documents; public summaries list the 26 September 2025 deadline.

title: BG16RFPR002-1.005 Small Innovative Grants for SMEs
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / RIDST 2021-2027
amount: Fixed grant BGN 14,710 for one service or BGN 29,420 for two services
eligibilitySummary: Closed Bulgarian fixed-grant route for SMEs buying science-intensive innovation services from eligible knowledge providers.
description: A reference procedure for Bulgarian SMEs needing focused help from universities, research organizations, competence centers, Sofia Tech Park R&D association, or scientific infrastructure providers.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises
Companies needing innovation services from eligible knowledge providers
Applicants with a defined technological or business-model problem
whoCannotApply: Companies without SME status
Applicants seeking general consulting unrelated to innovation
Projects without an eligible knowledge provider
eligibleCosts: New product, process, service, or business-model support
sourceNotes: MIG programme page lists BG16RFPR002-1.005 under R&I and Digitalisation for Smart Transformation.
